channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Americans are feeling inflation's pinch into the holiday weekend. Here's where prices are rising the most
item.description: Prices related to travel, recreation and food saw particularly steep increases, stretching Americans' wallets as they celebrate the unofficial start of summer.

item.title: Trump not rushing Iran deal, whacks critics as 'losers'
item.description: The president said the U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports would remain in effect until "an agreement is reached, certified, and signed."

item.title: 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' is Disney's lowest-ever Star Wars film opening
item.description: Disney's "The Mandalorian and Grogu" tallied and estimated $82 million in domestic ticket sales through its first three days in theaters.

item.title: Disney's 'Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu' tallies lowest Thursday preview sales in franchise history
item.description: Disney's "Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu" tallied $12 million in Thursday night previews, the lowest collection of advance tickets in franchise history.

item.title: These companies have cash and are liked on Wall Street
item.description: Wolfe Research screened for companies with market caps above $250 million that are in the top quintile for net cash-to-market cap ratio.

item.title: Suspect dead after opening fire near White House security checkpoint, Secret Service says
item.description: Secret Service said none of its officers were injured, and that President Donald Trump — who was at the White House at the time — was not "impacted."

item.title: 'Potential crack' in Southern California chemical tank may prevent explosion, fire official says
item.description: Although officials are focused on measuring pressure, the crack eventually could allow authorities to gradually drain the chemicals.

item.title: The Middle East war is testing the Gulf's ambitions to become an AI hub
item.description: Attacks on data centers in the Middle East and persistently high energy prices have altered the calculus for operators of the facilities, analysts say.

item.title: Russia hits Ukraine with hypersonic missile in one of the war's biggest attacks on Kyiv
item.description: The Oreshnik missile has a range of several thousand kilometers and is capable of carrying a nuclear warhead.

item.title: China tech plays to ride out macro volatility
item.description: The China stock play right now is to focus on artificial intelligence-related names, regardless of the slower economic growth, several analysts said.

item.title: Boosting retirement savings has a less-appreciated benefit
item.description: Saving more money has a dual benefit that often goes unnoticed, according to financial advisors.

item.title: Americans are 'entrenched' in financial stress amid debt and price pressures
item.description: Inflation and high debt levels continue cause financial anxiety for many Americans. Nonprofits can help with debt mangement.

item.title: Top Wall Street analysts like these dividend stocks for steady income
item.description: Amid ongoing uncertainty, investors seeking consistent income can consider adding dividend stocks to their portfolios.

item.title: Move over, seltzer. Non-carbonated drinks are taking the spotlight
item.description: Non-carbonated alcoholic drinks like Surfside and BeatBox are stealing "share of throat" from hard seltzers, particularly among Gen Z.

item.title: Here's the case for 9,000 on the S&P 500 by the middle of next year, according to JPMorgan
item.description: Higher productivity could be even more supportive of the stock market than previously thought.

item.title: 'I never heard of the Strait of Hormuz before this': How one medical supply CEO is navigating the oil price shock
item.description: Medical supply company Gentell sources raw materials from around the world, and the crisis at the Strait of Hormuz is causing volatility for its business.

item.title: Spirit's collapse, high fuel prices test limits of summer vacation spending
item.description: The unofficial start of the summer travel season on Memorial Day weekend will show just how much travelers want to fly and how much they're willing to pay.

item.title: I’ve worked with over 1,000 kids—the ones with the best people skills use these 6 phrases
item.description: "Strong people skills are not the same as people-pleasing," says child life specialist and therapist Kelsey Mora, who's supported thousands of kids and families.

item.title: Barriers grow for international students seeking U.S. jobs: The 'American dream ... is collapsing'
item.description: International graduates say a weak hiring market and changing immigration rules make it harder for them to achieve their American dream of working in the U.S.

item.title: ‘Blue dot fever’? What's really behind a tricky summer dynamic for live music
item.description: As prices rise everywhere, consumers are choosing to be more intentional with how they're spending on live music and concerts this year.

item.title: Bulls push the S&P 500 back near records — here’s what drove the market last week
item.description: The combination of higher oil prices and bond market volatility nearly snapped the S&P 500's winning streak.

item.title: What is a tax relief company?
item.description: How tax relief companies work, what they can (and can’t) do, and what to watch out for.

item.title: Nvidia says its forecast for $200 billion CPU market includes China
item.description: Nvidia's latest forecast signals it still sees significant long-term demand for central processing units.

item.title: Stephen Colbert returns to TV in appearance on local Michigan station
item.description: A day after his last episode of “The Late Show” on CBS, Colbert found his way back to a television hosting gig for one night.

item.title: Millennials spend $252 on an average date, BMO finds — and social media is spiraling over 'date-flation'
item.description: Dating costs are rising faster than inflation, pushing many Americans to go on fewer dates, choose cheaper plans or skip dating altogether.

item.title: Delivery Hero confirms takeover offer from Uber
item.description: Delivery Hero said last week its U.S. rival had increased its holding to about 19.5% of issued capital from roughly 7%, becoming its largest shareholder.

item.title: Kyle Busch died after severe pneumonia progressed into sepsis, his family says
item.description: Busch, a two-time NASCAR champion, died at 41 on Thursday, a day after passing out in a Chevrolet simulator.

item.title: U.S. adds Atlanta area airport for Ebola screening, CDC says
item.description: Washington's Dulles International Airport was designated this week to screen returning citizens for the Ebola virus.

item.title: 'Fjord,' Norway-set drama about political polarization, wins Palme d’Or at Cannes
item.description: "Fjord" found wide admiration for its engrossing tale of what Mungiu called "left-wing fundamentalism."

item.title: Three signs from APEC that the U.S. and China remain far apart on trade
item.description: U.S. and Chinese officials have met and spoken publicly about differing priorities since the Trump-Xi summit concluded in Beijing last week.
